goal.original: Continue MPI-64 RunPod remote engine; work the open app-side items. This session: picked MPI-73 (premature 'Connected' STARTING hang + Stop can't clear) and shipped it end-to-end.
goal.summary: MPI-73 is DONE, user-verified, and COMMITTED (6d17da0) on RunPod; its card moved doing->done (maturity complete). MPI-64 is the active card again with its remaining open items still open. Next session picks the next MPI-64 follow-up.

completed: MPI-73 SHIPPED + USER-VERIFIED + COMMITTED (6d17da0): (1) Bug 1 premature 'Connected' -> STARTING hang — readiness/generation now gated on the binary-preview WS actually being OPEN, not wrapper-health alone. comfyController: _wsReady + isWsReady() + ensureWsConnected() (retries across its window AND awaits remoteEngineClient.refresh() FIRST, because boot/Settings call it directly bypassing ensureServerRunning, so without the refresh wsUrl() is null and connect() falls back to the LOCAL ws -> never opens remote -> false 'almost ready'). onopen sets _wsReady, onclose/_onWsDropped clear it. _ensureRemoteReady refuses generation until WS open. Boot (shell.js) + Settings Connect gate 'ready' on the real handshake. (2) Bug 2 — Stop on a never-started CUE job (no prompt_id, exec promise never settles): cancelRunningCueJob detects !promptId, frees the stuck dispatch via extracted _finishActiveCueDispatch({skipNext}), clears pending queue (no re-promote into another hang); gallery+groupHistory PromptBox Stop route queue-managed targets through cancelRunningCueJob. (3) Connect/disconnect feedback — remote:connection gained a 'phase' (connecting|disconnecting|null); hero 'connecting · offline' / 'disconnecting · online' no GPU card; status bar 'IDLE · Connecting' / 'IDLE · Disconnecting'; shell.js centralizes via _emitRemoteConnection + _setRemotePhase (feed never strips the phase; mirrored into state.remoteEnginePhase). _renderGpu skips the local GPU line during a transition (late gpu-info fetch was re-painting over the cleared card at boot). (4) Cue-disable during a transition — MpiPromptBox reads state.remoteEnginePhase at mount (race-free for a late-mounted box) + on state:changed; disables the Cue button, hold-to-loop gesture, and run hotkey; ensureServerRunning backstop refusal demoted to a plain ui:info toast (not the bug-reporter modal). (5) Models hero stat re-syncs on the remote connected edge (boot check ran pre-connect -> stale N/N). New state key: state.remoteEnginePhase.

MPI-73 residual (accepted, own follow-up if it bites): ~30s between the Pod actually being ready and the app showing connected (wrapper-health poll cadence: 4s interval + 5s fetch timeout, plus the preview-WS handshake stacking). key_decisions: Connect readiness must gate on the binary-preview WS being OPEN, not wrapper-health 'ready' (ComfyUI up) alone — accepting a job before the WS handshake hangs it in STARTING with no prompt_id.

ensureWsConnected MUST await remoteEngineClient.refresh() first: boot/Settings call it directly (not via ensureServerRunning), so the remote ws token/base aren't loaded yet and connect() would fall back to the local ws -> never opens remote -> false 'almost ready' + hero stuck offline.

A never-started CUE job (no prompt_id) needs an explicit local settle: its exec promise never resolves, so the dispatcher slot is freed manually (_finishActiveCueDispatch{skipNext}) + the pending queue cleared, rather than re-promoting into another hang.

Cross-surface transition feedback uses a new canonical state key state.remoteEnginePhase (read at mount = race-free for late-mounted components) + the existing remote:connection event 'phase' field. shell.js _emitRemoteConnection is the single emitter that folds the phase into feed ticks so the feed never strips it.

Prevention > error: the Cue button is DISABLED during a transition (button + hold + hotkey); the ensureServerRunning refusal is only a backstop and now a plain info toast, never the bug-reporter modal.

Events-rules compliant: reused canonical remote:connection + state:changed; component subs stored in _unsubs + cleaned in destroy; app-lifetime singleton subs marked // eslint-disable-next-line mpi/require-destroy-on-events; top-level state assign (no sub-object mutation).
constraints: NEVER read/grep cubric-remote-wrapper/.secrets/runpod.env; NEVER run autonomous Pod create/delete or image builds (USER runs all live Pod ops + builds).
Backend logger (routes/logger.js) warn/info take only (category, message); clientLogger.warn (frontend) is ALSO (category, message) only — inline state into the message string.
Backend changes (routes/, server.js, main.js) need a FULL app restart; renderer changes (js/, css/) reload with Ctrl+R.
POD_IMAGE/WRAPPER_VERSION + in-process _mode consts need an APP RESTART. routes/remoteProxy.js MUST stay mounted before routes/comfy.js.

App quit with delete-on-quit UNCHECKED = STOP-not-delete (warm-resumable, keeps podId + wasConnected). Boot auto-reconnect gate: wasConnected && podId && gpuType && !deleteOnQuit. To test boot connect feedback: Connect -> quit (not Delete) -> restart.
mpi-ci (separate repo at c:/AI/Mpi/mpi-ci, branch main) holds the wrapper + Dockerfile. Commit+push BEFORE gh workflow run. Push is USER-run.
project-paths.json (repo root) is gitignored; never commit it.
Kanban task cards use schema mpi-kanban/task-card/v1; maturity enum = idea|planned|in-progress|validating|complete.
Commit only when the USER asks (this session the user authorized the MPI-73 commit + handoff).
